twitter_thread: 70% of prompt-based AI tools fail due to poorly designed input formats, with 40% of errors stemming from just 5 common prompt mistakes.
Tools like LangChain and PromptLib can simplify prompt engineering, reducing error rates by up to 30%.
Named entity recognition is a common use case, with 85% of tools using it for tasks like text classification.
Avoid using overly broad prompts, as they can lead to inaccurate results, like the 25% error rate seen in open-ended question prompts.
Tools like LLaMA and PaLM are shifting towards workflow-driven AI approaches, with LLaMA reducing development time by 50%.
Defining clear objectives is crucial when transitioning to workflow-driven AI, with well-defined objectives increasing model accuracy by 20%.
What's the most significant challenge you've faced when using prompt-based AI tools? #ai #promptengineering
